visits.note: Patient presents with hypertension, type 2 diabetes, and hyperlipidemia. Blood pressure is 145/90 mmHg, HbA1c is 7.5, and LDL is 135 mg/dL. The patient is started on metformin 500mg twice daily, lisinopril 10mg daily, and atorvastatin 20mg daily.

Follow-up visit to assess glycemic control and blood pressure. HbA1c has decreased to 7.0, but blood pressure has increased to 150/95 mmHg. LDL has decreased to 120 mg/dL. The patient is started on sitagliptin 100mg daily for diabetes.
diagnoses: Coronary Artery Disease (new diagnosis based on increased blood pressure)

visits.note: Follow-up visit to assess glycemic control, blood pressure, and lipid profile. HbA1c has increased to 7.8, but blood pressure has decreased to 140/90 mmHg. LDL has increased to 145 mg/dL. The patient is started on ramipril 10mg daily for hypertension.

Follow-up visit to assess glycemic control, blood pressure, and kidney function. HbA1c has decreased to 7.2, but eGFR has decreased to 60. The patient is started on sacubitril/valsartan 200/12.5mg daily for heart failure.
diagnoses: Chronic Kidney Disease (new diagnosis based on decreased eGFR)

visits.note: Follow-up visit to assess glycemic control, blood pressure, and kidney function. HbA1c has increased to 7.9, but eGFR has stabilized at 65. The patient is started on sodium-glucose cotransporter 2 (SGLT2) inhibitor, dapagliflozin 10mg daily, for diabetes.
